groups.desc: Questions 1-5 Choose the correct letter, A, B or C. Example 　　How long has Sally been waiting? 　　Ⓐ five minutes 　　Ⓑ twenty minutes 　　🅒 thirty minutes

questions.title: What does Peter want to drink?
questions.analysis: 正确答案是C. a cold drink，因为Peter明确表示他想要喝“a really chilled mineral water or something”，这是一种冷饮。学生在听力过程中需要特别注意定位词和信号词，抓住关键句，理解其中的意思，即可正确作答。

questions.title: What caused Peter problems at the bank?
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是C. The computers weren’t working。听力原文中明确提到“the computer system was temporarily down, so they couldn't do any transactions”，这表明问题的原因是计算机系统故障，无法进行交易。因此，选项C是正确的。

questions.title: Who did Peter talk to at the bank?
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B（an American man），因为听力原文明确提到Peter在银行遇到的那个人是“from New York”，而纽约在美国。通过定位词和信号词的分析，学生可以更容易地找到正确答案。即使基础较差的学生，也可以通过抓住关键词和使用排除法来提高答题的准确率。

questions.title: Henry gave Peter a map of
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B. the bus routes。听力原文中明确提到Henry给了Peter一张公交系统的地图（map of the bus system），这与选项B完全对应。题干中的定位词“Henry”和“map”在听力原文中也有明确的对应信号词。对于基础较差的学生，抓住关键词并通过排除法可以有效提高正确率。学生容易错选的选项主要是A和C，需要特别注意区分“bus system”和其他公共交通系统。

questions.title: What do Peter and Sally decide to order?
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A. food and drinks。通过分析对话内容，特别是Peter提到的“let's just have a snack and a drink”，可以明确他们决定点的是食物和饮料。学生在听力过程中应特别注意“order”这个定位词，并在听到相关信号词时，迅速捕捉到答案。

groups.desc:
Questions 6-8
Complete the notes below using words from the options given below.

questions.title: Tourist attractions open all day: _ and Gardens
questions.analysis: 根据听力原文，Cathedral（大教堂）每天都开放，虽然在周日可能因为教堂服务会更难进入，但并没有提到具体的开放时间限制，因此符合“open all day”的描述。

questions.title: Tourist attractions NOT open on Mondays: _ and Castle
questions.analysis: 本题要求找出周一不开放的旅游景点。根据听力原文，城堡在周一不开放，市场只在周四开放，因此市场在周一也不开放。其他选项中，艺术画廊在周日不开放，教堂每天都开放，花园每天都开放，只在晚上关闭。因此，正确答案是E. Markets。

questions.title: Tourist attractions which have free entry: _ and Markets
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是D. Gardens，因为听力原文明确提到“there's a charge for all of them except the Botanical Gardens”，即除了植物园外，其他地方都需要收费。Markets也被提到是免费的，因此答案是Gardens和Markets。通过定位词“free entry”和信号词“don't pay to go in”，可以准确找到答案。

groups.desc:
Questions 9 and 10
Complete the sentences below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.
groups.collect:
The first place Peter and Sally will visit is the $21840$.
At the Cathedral, Peter really wants to $21841$.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是“art gallery”，因为在听力原文中，Peter提到他们首先要去看Sally喜欢的画，这幅画是伦勃朗的作品，而伦勃朗的画在艺术馆中。因此，第一站是艺术馆。

答案是“climb the tower”，因为听力材料中明确提到“PETER: What I really want to do at the Cathedral is climb the tower”。考生需要注意题干中的定位词“Cathedral”和“really wants to”，并在听力过程中关注这些词汇的出现。即使基础较差的学生，也可以通过抓住关键词和利用上下文来猜测答案。避免易错点，特别是动词的选择和细节的忽略。

transcript.en: SALLY:Oh, PETER, there you are.
transcript.cn: 莎莉：噢，Peter，原来你在这儿啊。

transcript.en: You've been ages. What kept you so long?
transcript.cn: 已经过了很长时间了。什么事情耽搁了？

transcript.en: PETER:I'm sorry I'm so late, SALLY.
transcript.cn: 彼得：对不起，我迟到了，Sally。

transcript.en: Have you been waiting long?
transcript.cn: 你等很久了吗？

transcript.en: SALLY:Oh, half an hour.
transcript.cn: 莎莉：噢，半小时了。

transcript.en: But it doesn't matter.
transcript.cn: 但不要紧。

transcript.en: I've had a coffee and I've been reading this guidebook for tourists.
transcript.cn: 我喝了杯咖啡，还一直在读游客指南。

transcript.en: Sit down. You look very hot and tired.
transcript.cn: 快坐。你看起来很热又累。

transcript.en: What would you like to drink?
transcript.cn: 想喝点什么？

transcript.en: PETER:I'd love a really chilled mineral water or something.
transcript.cn: 彼得：我真想来点儿冰镇矿泉水什么的。

transcript.en: Will you have another coffee?
transcript.cn: 你要再来一杯咖啡吗？

transcript.en: SALLY:Yes. I will. The waitress will be back in a moment.
transcript.cn: 莎莉：是的。服务员一会儿就回来。

transcript.en: Why were you so late?
transcript.cn: 你为什么迟到了？

transcript.en: Did something happen?
transcript.cn: 发生了什么事吗？

transcript.en: PETER:Yes. You know I went to the bank to cash some travellers cheques?
transcript.cn: 彼得：是的，我去了银行兑现旅行支票。

transcript.en: Well, the exchange rate was looking healthy, but when I went to the teller, they told me the computer system was temporarily down, so they couldn't do any transactions.
transcript.cn: 汇率之前看起来很好，但我去了之后柜员告诉我电脑系统暂时出现故障，因此无法进行任何交易。

transcript.en: They said the problem would be fixed in a few minutes, so I waited.
transcript.cn: 他们说问题一会儿就可以解决，所以我等着。

transcript.en: And then I started talking to another guy in the bank, and I forgot the time.
transcript.cn: 然后我开始和另一个在银行的伙计聊天，忘了时间。

transcript.en: SALLY:Oh, really?
transcript.cn: 莎莉：哦，真的吗？

transcript.en: Someone you met in the bank?
transcript.cn: 你在银行遇见的人？

transcript.en: Does he work there?
transcript.cn: 他在那里工作吗？

transcript.en: PETER:No, he was a tourist, from New York.
transcript.cn: 彼得：不，他是游客，从纽约来的。

transcript.en: His name's Henry, and he's been here for a week, but he's moving on to Germany tomorrow.
transcript.cn: 他的名字是Henry，他在这儿已经一周了，但他明天要去德国。

transcript.en: He's an architect, and he's spending four weeks travelling around Europe.
transcript.cn: 他是一个建筑师，他准备花四周时间在欧洲旅行。

transcript.en: SALLY:Just like us!
transcript.cn: 莎莉：就像我们一样！

transcript.en: PETER:Yeah, just like us.
transcript.cn: 彼得：是的，就像我们一样。

transcript.en: He told me the names of some places where we should eat.
transcript.cn: 他告诉我了一些该去的餐厅的名字。

transcript.en: Great food, and not too expensive, he said.
transcript.cn: 他说那里的食物好吃而且不太贵。

transcript.en: Oh, and he also gave me this map of the bus system.
transcript.cn: 哦，它还给我了个公交系统地图。

transcript.en: He said he didn't need it anymore.
transcript.cn: 他说他不需要了。

transcript.en: SALLY:That's useful. Pity he's moving on tomorrow.
transcript.cn: 莎莉：这很有用。真遗憾他明天就走了。

transcript.en: Ah, here's the waitress. Let's order.
transcript.cn: 啊，女服务生来了，我们点餐吧。

transcript.en: Do you want anything to eat, or shall we just have a drink?
transcript.cn: 你想吃点什么吗？还是我们就只喝点东西？

transcript.en: PETER:Well. I'm hungry, and we've got a lot of sightseeing to do, so let's just have a snack and a drink.
transcript.cn: 彼得：好的。我饿了，而且我们要逛很久。所以来点儿小吃和饮料吧。

transcript.en: SALLY:Sounds good to me.
transcript.cn: 莎莉：这听起来不错。

transcript.en: PETER:Well, let's decide what we'll see today.
transcript.cn: 皮特：嗯，让我们来决定我们今天游览什么。

transcript.en: I guess the best place to start is the Cathedral, and then the Castle.
transcript.cn: 我觉得最好的游览起点是教堂，然后是城堡。

transcript.en: What are the opening times for those two?
transcript.cn: 那两个的开放时间是什么时候？

transcript.en: SALLY:Well, according to this guidebook, the Cathedral is only open from nine-thirty in the morning until midday.
transcript.cn: 莎莉：嗯，根据游览指南，教堂只从早上9：30分开放到中午。

transcript.en: No. hang on. That's the Cathedral Museum.
transcript.cn: 哦，不，等等，那是教堂博物馆的开放时间。

transcript.en: The Cathedral itself is open morning and afternoon.
transcript.cn: 教堂本身在早上和下午开放。

transcript.en: The Castle is just open from one to five, so we can't go there until after lunch.
transcript.cn: 城堡只从下午一点开到五点，所以我们直到吃过午餐后才能去。

transcript.en: I really want to spend some time in the Art Gallery, because they've got this wonderful painting by Rembrandt that I've always wanted to see.
transcript.cn: 我真的想花点时间待在艺术画廊，因为那里有我想一睹为快的伦勃朗的画。

transcript.en: PETER:What else should we see?
transcript.cn: 皮特：我们还应该去看什么？

transcript.en: SALLY:Well, the guidebook says the Botanical Gardens are worth spending some time in, and they're open all day, from eight to six, so we can go there any time.
transcript.cn: 莎莉：嗯，游览指南说植物园值得一花时间一去。那里全天8点到6点开放，所以我们随时可以去。

transcript.en: I'd like to go to the Markets near the river too, but... oh ... no, wait, that's only in the mornings, too.
transcript.cn: 我也想去河边的市场，但是...哦，不行，那只在早晨开。

transcript.en: PETER:As well as today and tomorrow, we can see some other places on Monday, you know.
transcript.cn: 皮特：不仅是今天和明天，我们可以在周一去看更多的地方。

transcript.en: But I don't think the Markets will be open then: they only open on Thursdays, so we've missed them for this week.
transcript.cn: 但是我觉得那是市场不会开。他们只在周四开。所以我们这周要错过了。

transcript.en: Maybe we should go to the Cathedral today because it's Sunday tomorrow, and even though it's open every day it might be more difficult to get in tomorrow because of the church services.
transcript.cn: 也许我们今天应该去教堂，因为明天是周日，即使它每天开放，明天也可能因为礼拜仪式更难进去。

transcript.en: SALLY:That's true, but the Art Gallery isn't open on Sundays at all, so we'll have to go there today.
transcript.cn: 莎莉：嗯，是的。但是艺术画廊周日根本不开，所以我们今天必须去。

transcript.en: The Castle's open every day except Mondays, so we're OK there, and the Gardens of course only close at night.
transcript.cn: 城堡除了周一每天都开放。所以那里是可以的，当然花园晚上是不开的。

transcript.en: PETER:Are all these places free or do we have to pay to go in?
transcript.cn: 皮特：所有这些地方都免费吗？还是需要付钱进去？

transcript.en: What does the guidebook say?
transcript.cn: 旅游指南上怎么说？

transcript.en: SALLY:I think there's a charge for all of them except the Botanical Gardens.
transcript.cn: 莎莉：我觉得除了植物园，其他都是收费的。

transcript.en: Oh, and the Markets, of course you don't pay to go in.
transcript.cn: 哦，还有市场，当然你不用花钱进入。

transcript.en: PETER:OK, well, it looks like our plan is this: we'll go to see the painting you like first, the Rembrandt, then have lunch and go on to the Castle after that, and then the Cathedral.
transcript.cn: 彼得：好吧，嗯，看来这就是我们的计划了。我们将会去看油画，你喜欢的先来，伦勃朗之类的，然后吃午饭，去城堡，最后去教堂。

transcript.en: SALLY:OK. It says here that the roof of the Cathedral is really beautiful.
transcript.cn: 莎莉：好的，听说教堂的穹顶非常漂亮。

transcript.en: PETER:Is that right?
transcript.cn: 彼得：真的吗？

transcript.en: What I really want to do at the Cathedral is climb the tower.
transcript.cn: 但是我真的想去教堂爬塔。

transcript.en: The view is supposed to be spectacular.
transcript.cn: 据说景色壮观极了。

transcript.en: SALLY:OK, well, that'll be more than enough for today.
transcript.cn: 莎莉：好吧，这样今天就足够了吧。

transcript.en: Then, tomorrow, let's go to the Botanical Gardens and have a picnic.
transcript.cn: 那么明天我们去植物园野餐吧。

transcript.en: I want to sit by the river and watch the swans.
transcript.cn: 我想坐在河边看着天鹅。

transcript.en: This city's famous for them.
transcript.cn: 这座城市因此而出名。

groups.desc:
Questions 11-20
Choose the correct letter A, B or C.

questions.title: The Counselling Service may contact tutors if

options.title: A
options.content: they are too slow in marking assignments

options.title: B
options.content: they give students a lot of work

options.title: C
options.content: they don’t inform students about their progress

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是C，因为听力原文明确提到“chase up your tutor if you're not getting proper feedback on how you are getting on in your subject”，这与题干中的“inform students about their progress”直接对应。通过定位词“contact tutors”和信号词“chase up your tutor”，可以准确找到答案句并确定答案。

questions.title: Stress may be caused by
questions.analysis: 正确答案是B，因为听力原文中明确提到了“mounting pressure as the deadline for that first assignment creeps up on you”，这直接对应了选项B中的“time pressure”。

questions.title: International students may find stress difficult to handle because

options.title: A
options.content: they lack support from family and friends

options.title: B
options.content: they don’t have time to make new friends

options.title: C
options.content: they find it difficult to socialise

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是A，因为听力材料明确提到国际学生在应对压力时缺乏原有的社交网络，包括家人和朋友的支持。通过定位词和信号词的分析，可以快速定位到相关信息，并排除其他不相关的选项。

questions.title: A personal crisis may be caused by

options.title: B
options.content: business problems in the student’s own country

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是C，因为听力原文明确提到“interrupted personal relationships or even sometimes have broken them off”是导致个人危机的原因。通过定位词“personal crisis”和信号词“trigger a personal crisis”，可以迅速找到相关信息并确定答案。

questions.title: Students may lose self-esteem if

options.title: C
options.content: their family puts too much pressure on them

questions.analysis: 正确答案是B，因为听力内容明确提到“exam failure can lead to worrying changes in the way you normally behave”，这与题干中的“lose self-esteem”相对应。考试失败会导致学生行为方式的变化，这实际上是自尊心受损的表现。

questions.title: Students should consult Glenda Roberts if

options.title: C
options.content: they can’t eat the local food

questions.analysis: 正确答案是C。因为原文中提到，如果学生因为当地食物不合口味而有饮食问题，可以咨询Glenda Roberts。选项A和B都没有提到与当地食物相关的问题。

questions.title: Students in financial difficulties can receive

options.title: B
options.content: a loan to pay their course fees

options.title: C
options.content: a no-interest loan to cover study expenses

questions.analysis: 正确答案是A。原文明确提到“full-time students can get a low-interest loan of up to six hundred dollars to buy books”，这与选项A完全吻合。题干中的“financial difficulties”在原文中被替换为“money problems”，而答案句中的“buy books”直接对应选项A。

questions.title: Loans are also available to students who
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是B，因为听力原文明确提到“starting-up expenses, including furniture for it, can be covered by a loan through the Welfare Service”。通过定位词“Loans”和“students”，以及信号词“loan”和“furniture”，可以准确找到答案句并确定答案。学生在听力过程中应特别注意这些关键词，并通过排除法和多次练习提高准确率。

questions.title: The number of students counselled by the service last year was
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是B. 240。通过定位词“number of students counselled”和信号词“last academic year, we counselled”，可以在听力材料中找到对应的答案句，并从中提取出答案词“two hundred and forty”。学生在听力过程中应特别注意数字和关键词，以便准确捕捉答案。

questions.title: The speaker thinks the Counselling Service

options.title: A
options.content: has been effective in spite of staff shortages

options.title: C
options.content: has suffered badly because of staff cuts

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A，因为听力原文明确提到尽管有人员削减，咨询服务仍然有效地帮助了很多学生，并且取得了不错的成绩。通过定位“Counselling Service”和信号词“in spite of staff cuts”，可以准确找到答案句并理解其含义。

transcript.en: So the counselling services we offer deal with any problems arising from your studies, or in your life outside the university.
transcript.cn: 我们的咨询服务，致力于你学习生活中或校园外产生的任何问题。

transcript.en: Let's take academic counselling.
transcript.cn: 我们先谈谈学业方面的咨询。

transcript.en: If you're confused about subjects or how to combine them in your degree, then we can advise you and discuss the career you are aiming for, so that you can see it all in context.
transcript.cn: 如果你对课程感到困惑，或者不知道如何把它们融入到你的学位中去，我们可以为你提供建议，并讨论你的职业规划。这样你就可以把这样的问题放到一个大环境下去考虑了。

transcript.en: We can also chase up your tutor if you're not getting proper feedback on how you are getting on in your subject.
transcript.cn: 如果你没有得到有关课程进展的合适反馈，我们也可以提醒你的导师。

transcript.en: Besides help with academic problems, you may also need personal counselling: if you think you're already under stress, well, just wait till classes begin next week.
transcript.cn: 除了帮助解决学术问题，你也许需要个人咨询。如果你觉得有压力，嗯，等到下周课程开始。

transcript.en: You'll have to start adjusting to teaching and learning methods that may be unfamiliar to you, as well as the mounting pressure as the deadline for that first assignment creeps up on you.
transcript.cn: 你要必须开始适应你不熟悉的教学与学习方法，以及逐渐增加的压力和第一份作业的截止日期。

transcript.en: And of course, you have to cope with all this without your usual social network - you know, the social contacts, family and friends you could normally rely on for help.
transcript.cn: 当然，你要必须学会在没有日常的社会关系网中处理所有事情，你知道的，交往的人，家庭和朋友，他们通常可以信赖和帮助你。

transcript.en: All of this causes anxiety.
transcript.cn: 所有这些都会造成焦虑。

transcript.en: Studying overseas can trigger a personal crisis - you may have left a lot of what you might call 'unfinished business' back in your own country, or you may have interrupted personal relationships or even sometimes have broken them off to come overseas, and so the student often feels lonely, unhappy, unmotivated and unable to concentrate on studying.
transcript.cn: 海外学习可能触发个人危机。你可能已经落下很多在祖国没有处理的事情。或者，你可能会因为出国而被阻断个人关系，或者甚至有时关系破裂的情况。所以，学生经常感到孤单，不开心，没有动力，不能集中精力到学习上。

transcript.en: Or there may be other things bothering you.
transcript.cn: 或存在其他困扰。

transcript.en: Our resident chaplain can offer you spiritual guidance if that's what you want, or we can put you in touch with community groups that can provide you with social contacts and friendship.
transcript.cn: 如果你想要（这些服务），我们的常驻牧师可以给你提供精神指导，我们可以和你的社区团体保持联系，为你提供社交联谊。

transcript.en: What about exam stress?
transcript.cn: 关于考试压力呢？

transcript.en: It affects nearly everyone to some extent, but especially overseas students like yourselves.
transcript.cn: 在某种程度上，它几乎影响每个人，但尤其是像你们这样的海外学生。

transcript.en: There may be a huge amount of family pressure on you to succeed, and if you fail a subject or drop out of a course because it's too difficult then your self- esteem can suffer.
transcript.cn: 你可能为了成功，身上有巨大的家庭负担。而且如果你挂了科或未完成学业是因为它太难，然后自尊心可能受挫。

transcript.en: But it's not the end of the world if you don't pass an exam -I had to resit First Year Anthropology, so I can certainly offer you a sympathetic ear!
transcript.cn: 但是考试没过并不是什么世界末日。我大一学人类学时也曾补考过，所以我会很乐意倾听你们的遭遇。

transcript.en: Anyway, exam failure can lead to worrying changes in the way you normally behave.
transcript.cn: 总之，考试失败可能导致你日常行为出现令人担忧的变化。

transcript.en: You may also be off your food, or you may have dietary problems because the local food is not to your liking and upsets you, and this can affect your health and studies.
transcript.cn: 你可能会不再有胃口，或因不喜欢当地食物而产生饮食问题，让你沮丧，这些可能影响你的健康和学习。

transcript.en: Glenda Roberts is our dietician in the Health Service and we can put you on to her.
transcript.cn: Glenda Roberts是我们的健康服务饮食专家，有什么问题可以问她。

transcript.en: And we all have money problems, don't we?
transcript.cn: 我们都有资金问题，对吗？

transcript.en: But remember, full-time students can get a low -interest loan of up to six hundred dollars to buy books and for similar study-related expenses.
transcript.cn: 但是记住，全日制学生可获得一笔600元的低息贷款，用来买书或支付其他相关学习费用。

transcript.en: That's right, and you can get double that amount if you can't afford an item of equipment you need for your course - a musical instrument, for example.
transcript.cn: 对。如果你买不起课程需要的器材，例如乐器，你可以得到双倍数量的贷款。

transcript.en: And it doesn't stop there.
transcript.cn: 还不止这些。

transcript.en: When you move into a flat, starting-up expenses, including furniture for it, can be covered by a loan through the Welfare Service - see Jill Freeman for details.
transcript.cn: 当你搬进公寓，所有福利服务涵盖了起始花费，包括家具。看看Jill Freeman具体怎么说。

transcript.en: Can we help you?
transcript.cn: 我们能够帮你吗？

transcript.en: Well, last academic year, in spite of staff cuts, we counselled two hundred and forty international students for a total of twenty-six hundred hours counselling, and, finally we won all but just one of the twelve appeals that we launched on behalf of students.
transcript.cn: 上个学年，尽管人手不够，我们对240个国际学生进行了咨询，共2600小时。我们代表学生们提起了12宗申诉，除了一宗之外，最终全部获胜。

transcript.en: Not too bad for an understaffed service, don't you think?
transcript.cn: 在人手不够的情况下不算太坏吧？

transcript.en: That's all from me. Thank you.
transcript.cn: 就这样，谢谢。

groups.title: DETAILS OF ASSIGNMENT
groups.desc:
Questions 21-24
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect: Part 1      Essay:                 Title ‘Assess the two methods of $21852$ in social science research                 Number of words: $21853$ Part 2      Small scale study:                 Choose one method                 Gather data from at least $21854$ subjects Part 3      Report on study:                 Number of words: $21855$

questions.analysis: 答案是“collecting data”，因为听力原文中明确提到“Assess the two main methods of collecting data in social science research”。通过定位词“two methods”和信号词“two main methods”，可以快速找到答案所在的句子。
题干要求填写的是“Number of words”，即字数。在听力原文中，MICK明确提到“Fifteen hundred words. That's for the essay.”，这句话直接回答了需要写多少字的问题。因此，正确答案是“1500”。
这道题的正确答案是“5”，因为听力材料中明确提到“we only need data from five subjects anyway”。学生需要通过定位词“Gather data”和信号词“data from five subjects”来准确找到答案。对于基础较差的学生，抓住关键词和数字是关键，同时要注意避免被其他数字干扰。
这道题的正确答案是“3000-4000”，因为听力原文中明确提到报告的字数是“three to four thousand words”。题干中的定位词是“Number of words”，在听力过程中需要特别注意与之相关的信号词“three to four thousand words”。通过这种方法，即使基础较差的学生也能较准确地找到答案。

groups.title: What TWO disadvantages of the questionnaire form of data collection do the students discuss?
groups.desc:
Questions 25 and 26
Choose TWO letters A-E.

list.title: C
list.content: It is less likely to reveal the unexpected

list.title: D
list.content: It can only be used with literate populations

list.title: E
list.content: There is a delay between the distribution and return of questionnaires

collect_option.title: What TWO disadvantages of the questionnaire form of data collection do the students discuss?

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B和C。学生讨论了问卷形式的数据收集的两个缺点：响应率可能太低（B选项）和问卷数据往往不会揭示任何意外的东西，因为它仅限于预先设定的问题（C选项）。
这道题的正确答案是B和C。学生讨论了问卷形式的数据收集的两个缺点：响应率可能太低（B选项）和问卷数据往往不会揭示任何意外的东西，因为它仅限于预先设定的问题（C选项）。

groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
Complete the table below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS OR A NUMBER for each answer.

content: sample surveys in social science research

questions.analysis: 答案是“Mehta”，因为在听力原文中，Mick明确提到书名《Sample Surveys in Social Science Research》后，紧跟着说“By Mehta”，这表明Mehta是这本书的作者。题目要求填写作者名，因此正确答案是“Mehta”。
答案是“Survey Research”，因为在听力原文中明确提到Bell所著的书名是“Survey Research”。通过定位词“Bell”和信号词“recommended”, “book”, “called”，可以在听力原文中找到对应的答案句。学生需要注意拼写正确，并且在听力过程中集中注意力捕捉信号词，以确保不遗漏关键信息。
在听力原文中，提到 Bell 的书时，紧接着提到了书名 "Survey Research"，并且明确指出这本书是由 "London University" 出版的。因此，正确答案是 "London University"。
答案是1988，因为在听力材料中明确提到《Interviews That Work》这本书是由Wilson编写，并且出版于1988年。通过定位词“Wilson”和信号词“Interviews That Work”，我们可以准确找到答案。

transcript.en: ROSA:Oh, there you are, good.
transcript.cn: 罗萨：哦，你在这儿啊，不错。

transcript.en: Sorry I'm a bit late - there was a long queue.
transcript.cn: 对不起，有点迟了。排了很长的队。

transcript.en: So, have you worked out how to deal with this assignment then?
transcript.cn: 那么，你有没有想出如何处理这项任务呢？

transcript.en: MICK:Not yet, we've only been here a couple of minutes ourselves.
transcript.cn: 米克：还没有，我们在这里待了没多久。

transcript.en: ROSA:Can you just remind me what the task is exactly?
transcript.cn: 罗萨：你能再提醒我一下到底是什么任务吗？

transcript.en: PETE:Well, there are two, no, three, parts to it: first, we've got to write an essay about ways of collecting data.
transcript.cn: 皮特：嗯，有两个部分，哦不，是三个。第一，我们需要写一个关于数据收集的小文章，

transcript.en: Then ...
transcript.cn: 然后…

transcript.en: ROSA:What's the title of the essay exactly?
transcript.cn: 罗萨：确切的论文题目是什么？

transcript.en: MICK:I've got it here: 'Assess the two main methods of collecting data in social science research'.
transcript.cn: 米克：我已经有一个题目了：评定在社会科学研究中的两种收集数据的方法。

transcript.en: ROSA:And how much do we need to write?
transcript.cn: 罗萨：我们要写多少字？

transcript.en: MICK:Fifteen hundred words.
That's for the essay.
transcript.cn: 那是论文的字数。

transcript.en: Then, for the second part of the assignment, we have to choose one method of data collection, and 'carry out a small-scale study, making appropriate use of the method chosen to gather data from at least five subjects'.
transcript.cn: 然后对这个任务的第二部分是我们必须选择一个数据收集的方法，适当实施一项小规模的研究方法，至少从5个主题中获取数据。

transcript.en: ROSA:And then we have to write a report on the study?
transcript.cn: 罗萨：然后我们要写一个关于研究的报告吗？

transcript.en: PETE:That's right, of three to four thousand words.
ROSA:Did you get as far as discussing which form of data collection we should go for - questionnaire or interview , isn't it?
transcript.cn: 罗萨：你们已经讨论了调查问卷和采访这两宗数据收集方法之中我们选择哪种这个问题了，是不是？

transcript.en: MICK:Yeah.
transcript.cn: 米克：是的。

transcript.en: I think we should use a questionnaire.
transcript.cn: 我觉得我们应该做调查问卷。

transcript.en: It'll be so much less time-consuming than organising interviews,I reckon.
transcript.cn: 我觉得那会比组织面试更节省时间。

transcript.en: Once we've agreed on the wording of it, we only have to send it out and wait for the responses.
transcript.cn: 一旦我们已经达成一致，就只需发出去等待回应。

transcript.en: ROSA:Yes.
transcript.cn: 罗萨：是的。

transcript.en: I think it probably would be quicker.
transcript.cn: 我觉得那样可能更快一些。

transcript.en: But what did that article he gave us last week say about the quality of data from questionnaires?
transcript.cn: 但上周给我们的那篇文章说了关于问卷中数据质量什么？

transcript.en: MICK:I'm pretty sure it recommended questionnaires as a source of 'highly reliable data'.
transcript.cn: 米克：我非常确定，推荐的问卷是有效数据的来源。

transcript.en: As long as you design the questionnaire properly in the first place, the data will be fine.
transcript.cn: 只要一开始就设计出正确的问卷，数据不会有问题的。

transcript.en: ROSA:No.
transcript.cn: 罗萨：不，

transcript.en: I'm sure it talked about drawbacks as well, didn't it?
transcript.cn: 我确定它还谈论了一些缺陷，不是吗？

transcript.en: Something about the response rate and the problems you get if it's too low.
transcript.cn: 一些关于回应率低的问题。

transcript.en: MICK:Yeah but we only need data from five subjects anyway.
transcript.cn: 米克：是的，但总之，我们只有5个主题需要数据。

transcript.en: ROSA:I suppose so.
transcript.cn: 罗萨：哦，似乎是这样。

transcript.en: Another drawback I remember it mentioned was that questionnaire data tends not to reveal anything unexpected, because it is limited to the questions fixed in advance by the researcher.
transcript.cn: 我记得它提到的另外一个缺陷在于，调查问卷不太可能揭示出乎意料的东西，因为问卷仅限于研究者事先确定好的问题。

transcript.en: MICK:Come on, ROSA.
transcript.cn: 米克：别这样，罗萨。

transcript.en: This is only a practice.
transcript.cn: 这只是一个练习而已。

transcript.en: It's not meant to be real research, is it?
transcript.cn: 它原本就不是真正的研究，不是吗？

transcript.en: ROSA:Well.
transcript.cn: 罗萨：好吧。

transcript.en: I'm not sure about that.
transcript.cn: 我不是很确定。

transcript.en: ROSA:Maybe I'd belter go through the article again, just to be sure.
transcript.cn: 罗萨：或许我我应该再读一遍那篇文章，好弄清楚到底怎么回事。

transcript.en: Can you remember what it was called?
transcript.cn: 你能记住它的名字吗？

transcript.en: MICK:'Sample Surveys in Social Science Research", I think.
transcript.cn: 米克：样本调查和社会科学研究，我认为。

transcript.en: By Mehta.
transcript.cn: Mehta写的。

transcript.en: ROSA:M-E-H-T-A?
transcript.cn: 罗萨：M-E-H-T-A？

transcript.en: MICK:Yeah.
transcript.cn: 米克：是的。

transcript.en: And he also recommended a more recent book, called 'Survey Research', by Bell, I think.
transcript.cn: 我认为他还推荐了一本更近代的书，是Belle写的《调查研究》。

transcript.en: It's in that series published by London University.
transcript.cn: 它是伦敦大学出版的艺术系列书籍。

transcript.en: PETE:And if we tried to use interviews instead.
transcript.cn: 皮特：如果我们试着用访谈法，

transcript.en: I saw a book in the departmental library that'll be helpful: it's called 'Interviews That Work', by Wilson, published in Oxford in nineteen eighty-eight.
transcript.cn: 我在部门图书馆看了一本书，Wilson编写牛津1988年出版的《Interviews》，这会对我们非常有用。

transcript.en: ROSA:Right.
transcript.cn: 罗萨：哦，

transcript.en: I've got a tutorial now.
transcript.cn: 我现在有一节指导课，

transcript.en: Can we meet up again later this week?
transcript.cn: 要不这周晚些时候我们再碰一次面？

transcript.en: What about Friday morning?
transcript.cn: 星期五上午怎么样？

transcript.en: PETE:Suits me.
transcript.cn: 皮特：好的。

transcript.en: Eleven o'clock?
transcript.cn: 十一点？

transcript.en: ROSA:: Fine.
transcript.cn: 罗萨：好的。

transcript.en: MICK:Before Friday, I think we should all look through the reading list.
transcript.cn: 米克：周五之前，我觉得我们应该看完书单的所有内容。

groups.desc:
Questions 31 and 32
Choose the correct letter A, B and C.

questions.title: Corporate crime is generally committed
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是C. for companies。听力原文明确指出公司犯罪是为了公司的利益，而不是针对个人或由团体实施的。通过抓住定位词“Corporate crime”和信号词“for the good of the company”，可以准确定位到答案。对于基础较差的学生，建议集中注意力听到关键短语，并使用排除法来选择正确答案。

questions.title: Corporate crime does NOT include
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是A. employees stealing from their company。听力材料明确指出，员工盗窃（如挪用公款或对雇主的欺诈）不包括在corporate crime的定义内。通过抓住定位词和信号词，学生可以更准确地定位到相关信息，从而做出正确的选择。

groups.desc:
Questions 33-38
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect:
Corporate crime has been ignored by:
    a) the $21864$ e.g. films
    b) $21865$

Reasons:
    a) often more complex and needing $21866$
    b) less human interest than conventional crime
    c) victims often $21867$

Effects:
    a) Economic costs
        • may appear unimportant to $21868$
        • can make large $21869$ for company
        • cause more losses to individuals than conventional crimes
    b) Social costs
        • make people lose trust in business world
        • affect poorer people most

questions.analysis: 正确答案是“mass media”，因为听力原文明确提到“Corporate crime has been relatively ignored by the mass media”，并且给出了具体的例子如新闻广播和电影。这与题干中的“ignored”和“e.g. films”相对应，表明大众媒体是忽视企业犯罪的主体。
答案是“academic circles”，因为听力材料中明确提到“it also tends to be ignored in academic circles”。题干中的定位词是“ignored”，在听力原文中也出现了相同的词，这提示我们答案即将出现。通过上下文可以确定，学术界是被忽视的对象，因此答案是“academic circles”。

正确答案是“specialist knowledge”，因为听力原文中明确提到“It's often very complex, whereas with conventional crime it's usually possible to follow what's going on without specialist knowledge.”。题干中的“complex”是定位词，听到这个词后，考生应特别注意后面的内容，最终找到“specialist knowledge”作为答案。
答案是“unaware”，因为听力原文明确提到“the victims are unaware”，并且重复了两次。题干中的定位词是“Victims”，在听力过程中，考生应特别注意这个词的出现，并关注其后的描述。对于基础较差的学生，抓住定位词和重复出现的关键词是关键。易错点在于可能会写成“unaware of”或“ignorant”，但应以原文中的“unaware”为准。

答案是“individual customers”，因为听力原文明确提到“small amounts like this may seem insignificant for individual customers”，这与题干中的“May appear unimportant to”直接对应。通过定位词“Economic costs”和信号词“insignificant”，可以准确找到答案所在的句子。学生需要注意听力中的关键描述，并理解题干的要求，才能正确填空。

答案是“illegal profits”，因为题干中提到“Can make large 【 】 for company”，其中“large”对应原文中的“massive”，“company”在原文中也有提到。原文中提到“this deception might result in massive illegal profit”，说明公司通过欺骗行为可以获得巨大的非法利润。因此，正确答案是“illegal profits”。

groups.title: The oil tanker explosion was an example of a crime which
groups.desc:
Questions 39 and 40
Choose TWO letters A-F.

list.title: F
list.content: made a large profit for the company

collect_option.title: The oil tanker explosion was an example of a crime which

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是D和E，因为听力内容明确提到油轮爆炸是由于公司对可能结果的冷漠（indifference）导致的，并且结果是非常悲惨的（serious consequences）。通过定位词和信号词的分析，学生可以更有效地在听力过程中捕捉到关键信息，从而做出正确的选择。
本题的正确答案是D和E，因为听力内容明确提到油轮爆炸是由于公司对可能结果的冷漠（indifference）导致的，并且结果是非常悲惨的（serious consequences）。通过定位词和信号词的分析，学生可以更有效地在听力过程中捕捉到关键信息，从而做出正确的选择。

transcript.en: So far, in these lectures, we've been looking at crimes like robbery and murder - both from a historical viewpoint and also in contemporary society - and we've seen that the preoccupation in Western society with crime and with lawlessness is part of a long and continuous tradition, rather than something which is new and unique to modern society.
transcript.cn: 到目前为止，在这些讲座中，我们从历史的角度和当代社会的角度上已经看到了很多种犯罪，例如抢劫和谋杀。我们看到在西方社会关注犯罪与不法行为是长期持续的传统的一部分，而不是现代社会的新生事物。

transcript.en: But over the past seventy years or so, there has been a massive increase in one type of crime, which is what's known as corporate crime'.
transcript.cn: 但是在过去的70年中，有一种犯罪呈大量增长趋势，这就是企业犯罪。

transcript.en: Corporate crime is crime which, as the name suggests, is connected withcompanies, with business organisations.
transcript.cn: 企业犯罪，顾名思义是与公司和商业组织有关。

transcript.en: It includes illegal acts of either individuals or a group within the company, but what is important is that these acts are normally in accordance with the goals of the company - they're for the good of the company rather than the individual.
transcript.cn: 它包括公司内个人或群体的不法行为。但重要的是这些行为通常与公司的目标一致。他们是为了公司而不是个人的利益。

transcript.en: It's been defined as, quote, 'crime which is committed for the corporate organisation' - the company - 'not against it', unquote.
transcript.cn: 它是这样定义的，“企业犯罪是为了公司的利益，而不是违背公司的利益。”

transcript.en: So crimes liketheft by employees- things like embezzlement or fraud against one's actual employer are excluded according to this definition.
transcript.cn: 所以根据这一定义，它不包括员工的偷窃、盗用公款和欺诈上级的行为在内。

transcript.en: The employees may be involved but they're acting in the first place for the company - they may not even realise they're committing a crime or they may realise but they feel it's excusable because it's policy , or because otherwise they may lose their jobs.
transcript.cn: 其中也许涉及员工自己，但是他们的行为首先是为了公司。他们可能甚至没有意识到自己在犯罪，或者他们知道，但是觉得根据公司政策可以被原谅，又或者他们反抗的话，就会失业。

transcript.en: So here , really , we're talking about the links between power and crime.
transcript.cn: 所以我们在这里真正谈论的是权力和犯罪的关系。

transcript.en: Now, this is one area that much less is generally known about than conventional or traditional crime.
transcript.cn: 相比传统犯罪来说，这是人们鲜为人知的一部分。

transcript.en: It has been relatively ignored by the mass media - for example, it tends to be under-reported in comparison with conventional crime in news broadcasts, and in crime serials and films and so on - they very rarely deal with corporate crime.
transcript.cn: 大众媒体也相对忽视了这部分。例如，对比传统犯罪在新闻广播、犯罪期刊和电影等媒介中的报道，这方面往往被报道得很少。他们很少讨论企业犯罪。

transcript.en: And it also tends to be ignored in academic circles - there's been far more research on conventional crime and far more data is available.
transcript.cn: 而且学术界也常常忽略这方面，只是过于研究传统犯罪，对此有大量的数据可循。

transcript.en: There are several reasons for this lack of interest in corporate crime, compared with other types of crime.
transcript.cn: 对企业犯罪兴趣的研究不及其他犯罪形式有几点原因。

transcript.en: It's often very complex, whereas with conventional crime it's usually possible to follow what's going on without specialist knowledge.
transcript.cn: 它往往很复杂，而传统犯罪，不需要专业知识也能理解发生了什么。

transcript.en: As well as this, whereas conventional crime usually has a lot of human interest, corporate crime often has much less.
transcript.cn: 除此之外，传统犯罪通常涉及很多人的利益，而企业犯罪在这方面涉及的很少。

transcript.en: The third reason, and possibly the most significant one, is that very often the victims are unaware - they think their misfortune is an accident or that it's the fault of no-one in particular.
transcript.cn: 第三个原因，可能是最重要的一个原因，受害者通常毫无察觉。他们认为自己的不幸是一件意外，或者不是特定某个人的错误。

transcript.en: They're unaware that they've been victims of a crime.
transcript.cn: 他们没有意识到自己成为了犯罪的受害者。

transcript.en: So, when we look at the effects of corporate crime we may find it's very difficult to assess the costs.
transcript.cn: 所以当我们看企业犯罪的影响时，我们可能发现很难评估其损失，

transcript.en: But these costs can be very considerable in both their economic and social aspects.
transcript.cn: 但是在经济和社会方面的损失是巨大的。

transcript.en: Let's look at the economic costs first.
transcript.cn: 让我们先看看经济上的损失。

transcript.en: For example, if a company is producing fruit juice and it dilutes its product so that it's just a little below the concentration it should be, many millions of people may be paying a small amount extra for their carton of orange juice.
transcript.cn: 例如，如果一个果汁公司在生产中稀释它的产品，使产品略低于本应达到的浓度，那么数百万人在买他们的盒装橙汁的时候就会多付一点钱。

transcript.en: Now small amounts like this may seem insignificant for individual customers - too small to worry about - but for the company this deception might result in massive illegal profit.
transcript.cn: 这样一小部分对个人客户来说似乎显得无关紧要，不值得引起人们的担心。但是对于公司来说，这种欺诈可以带来巨大的非法收入。

transcript.en: However, all studies of corporate crime agree that the individuals are in fact deprived of far more money by such crime than they are by conventional crime like robbery and theft.
transcript.cn: 然而，我们所有企业犯罪的研究表明，这种类型的个人犯罪其实比传统的抢劫、偷盗类犯罪要侵吞更大的资金。

transcript.en: In addition to this, we have to consider the social costs of corporate crime and these are again very difficult to assess, but they are considerable.
transcript.cn: 另外，我们还得考虑企业犯罪造成的社会代价，而且这些也非常难以评估，但是数量可观。

transcript.en: They're important because they can undermine the faith of the public in the business world and also, more importantly, because the main group of people they affect are, in fact, not the richer sections of society but the poorer - so here companies are robbing the poor to benefit the rich.
transcript.cn: 它们很重要，因为它们可以在商业界破坏公众的信心。另外，更重要的是因为它们影响的主要社会群体是穷人，而不是富人。所以在这一点上，公司通过榨取穷人，满足富人。

transcript.en: There are two more points to do with corporate crime that I'd like to illustrate with reference to a specific event which occurred several years ago.
transcript.cn: 还有两点跟企业犯罪有关，我将以几年前发生的具体案件为例，加以说明。

transcript.en: This was an explosion of a large oil tanker which caused the loss of more than fifty lives of the crew.
transcript.cn: 这是一场大型油轮爆炸，造成50多名船员丧生。

transcript.en: It was an explosion which never should have happened and a subsequent Inquiry laid the blame not on anyone who had actually been on the tanker at the time, but on the owners of the tanker.
transcript.cn: 这是一起本不应该发生的爆炸，随后的调查没有将责任归咎于当时实际在油轮上的人，而是归咎于这只油轮的所有者。

transcript.en: They had deliberately decided not to carry out necessary repair work on the tanker as it was due to be sold, and it was this lack of repair work which was directly responsible for the explosion.
transcript.cn: 他们故意决定不对油轮进行必要的维修工作，因为它马上要售出了。正是缺少维修工作才直接导致了爆炸。

transcript.en: Now this illustrates two points to do with corporate crime.
transcript.cn: 这说明了与企业犯罪有关的两点。

transcript.en: First of all, that it does not have to be intentional.
transcript.cn: 首先，不能是故意行为。

transcript.en: The owners of the tanker certainly did not intend it to explode.
transcript.cn: 油船的主人肯定不是蓄意让它引爆。

transcript.en: But very serious consequences can result from people or organisations not considering the possible results of their actions seriously enough.
transcript.cn: 但是人们和公司不计后果的行为可以造成严重的后果，他们的行为足够严重了。

transcript.en: The main crime here was (Q39&40)indifference to the human results rather than actual intention to harm anyone, but that didn't make the result any less tragic.
transcript.cn: 本案中主要的过错是对于可能造成人身伤害的结果的漠视，而不是真正要去伤害别人, 但这并没有使悲剧性的结果有任何改变。

transcript.en: And this leads me to my second point - that corporate crime can have very severe consequences.
transcript.cn: 这也就引向了我的第二个观点，企业犯罪可能导致很严重的后果。

transcript.en: It's not just a matter of companies making bigger profits than they should do, but of events which may affect the lives of innocent people, and yet very often companies, because they say they didn't intend to harm anyone, can avoid taking responsibility for the results of their actions.
transcript.cn: 这不仅仅是一个公司获得比应得的要多的利益问题，而是可能会影响到无辜人性命的事情。然而，因为公司通常会说他们不是故意伤人，所以他们可以免于为自己的所作所为负责，

transcript.en: And that has been a very dangerous loophole in the law.
transcript.cn: 这也是法律上的一个非常危险的漏洞。

transcript.en: A further example of corporate crime was...
transcript.cn: 企业犯罪的另一个例子是……

transcript.en: (fade out)
transcript.cn: （淡出）
